groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Business identity, post type, goal, message, local focus, audience, CTA, destination, and language for a valid first result.

groups.title: Offer or Event Details
groups.description: Structured details required only for Offer or Event workflows.

groups.title: Local Fit & Brand
groups.description: Geographic relevance, business voice, tone, and wording restrictions.

groups.title: Output Options
groups.description: Variant count, visual guidance, and final requirements.

variables.label: Business name
variables.placeholder: Example: Green Street Dental
variables.hint: Enter the public-facing name used on the Business Profile.

help.what: Enter the public-facing name used on the Business Profile.
help.why: The post must clearly represent the correct business.
help.example: Example: Green Street Dental
help.avoid: Avoid internal names, legal names not used publicly, or invented business names.

variables.label: Business category or service
variables.placeholder: Example: Family dental clinic or emergency plumbing
variables.hint: Describe what the business does in a few words.

help.what: Describe what the business does in a few words.
help.why: It helps the model use relevant terminology and understand the customer context.
help.example: Example: Family dental clinic or emergency plumbing
help.avoid: Avoid stuffing several unrelated services or claiming a category the business does not use.

variables.label: Post type
variables.placeholder: Choose a post type
variables.hint: Select the format that matches what you are publishing.

help.what: Select the format that matches what you are publishing.
help.why: Each native post type requires a different structure and set of details.
help.example: Choose a post type
help.avoid: Avoid choosing Offer or Event unless you have real structured details to publish.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important outcome for this post.

help.what: Choose the single most important outcome for this post.
help.why: The primary goal determines the post’s emphasis and CTA.
help.example: Choose the main goal
help.avoid: Avoid mixing unrelated objectives such as an offer, event, hiring post, and review request in one update.

variables.label: Key message and facts
variables.placeholder: Describe what happened, what is available, and the facts customers need to know.
variables.hint: Include only confirmed information that may appear in the post.

help.what: Include only confirmed information that may appear in the post.
help.why: It provides the factual foundation for the customer-facing content.
help.example: Describe what happened, what is available, and the facts customers need to know.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, prices, dates, availability, ratings, reviews, testimonials, or guarantees.

variables.label: Location or service area
variables.placeholder: Example: Central Bristol or serving northern Warsaw
variables.hint: Add a city, neighborhood, branch, or confirmed service area when relevant.

help.what: Add a city, neighborhood, branch, or confirmed service area when relevant.
help.why: It enables natural local relevance without invented geographic claims.
help.example: Example: Central Bristol or serving northern Warsaw
help.avoid: Avoid fake local references, unsupported service areas, or adding nearby cities for SEO only.

variables.label: Target customers
variables.placeholder: Example: Local families, commuters, homeowners, or restaurant guests
variables.hint: Describe the people most likely to find this update useful.

help.what: Describe the people most likely to find this update useful.
help.why: It helps prioritize the information and language most relevant to likely customers.
help.example: Example: Local families, commuters, homeowners, or restaurant guests
help.avoid: Avoid sensitive targeting assumptions or audiences unrelated to the actual local post.

variables.label: Preferred action
variables.placeholder: Choose an action
variables.hint: Auto selects one suitable action from the goal and supplied facts.

help.what: Auto selects one suitable action from the goal and supplied facts.
help.why: It defines the single conversion action without requiring marketing expertise.
help.example: Choose an action
help.avoid: Avoid multiple competing actions or a CTA that the profile cannot support.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/book
variables.hint: Add the exact page customers should reach after selecting the action.

help.what: Add the exact page customers should reach after selecting the action.
help.why: Link-based actions require an accurate destination that the model must not invent.
help.example: https://example.com/book
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, suspicious, or unrelated URLs.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish, or Polish
variables.hint: Enter the language in which the complete result should be written.

help.what: Enter the language in which the complete result should be written.
help.why: The publishing language cannot always be safely inferred from the business information.
help.example: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish, or Polish
help.avoid: Avoid mixing languages unless that is the established publishing approach.

variables.label: Offer or event title
variables.placeholder: Example: Summer Cleaning Offer or Local Skills Workshop
variables.hint: Enter a concise, factual title for the offer or event.

help.what: Enter a concise, factual title for the offer or event.
help.why: Offers and events require a clear structured title distinct from the description.
help.example: Example: Summer Cleaning Offer or Local Skills Workshop
help.avoid: Avoid changing the factual meaning of the supplied offer or event title.

variables.label: Dates, times, and time zone
variables.placeholder: Example: 18–25 July 2026, 09:00–18:00, Europe/Kyiv
variables.hint: Provide the complete confirmed schedule and time zone.

help.what: Provide the complete confirmed schedule and time zone.
help.why: Timing is essential for accurately publishing an offer or event.
help.example: Example: 18–25 July 2026, 09:00–18:00, Europe/Kyiv
help.avoid: Avoid relative dates, missing time zones, or invented deadlines.

variables.label: Offer value or benefit
variables.placeholder: Example: 20% off selected services or free initial consultation
variables.hint: State the exact confirmed benefit without adding promotional exaggeration.

help.what: State the exact confirmed benefit without adding promotional exaggeration.
help.why: It tells customers exactly what the promotion provides.
help.example: Example: 20% off selected services or free initial consultation
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported discounts, fake scarcity, false reference prices, or unclear benefits.

variables.label: Coupon code
variables.placeholder: Example: LOCAL20
variables.hint: Enter the exact code, including capitalization and symbols.

help.what: Enter the exact code, including capitalization and symbols.
help.why: A supplied redemption code must be preserved accurately.
help.example: Example: LOCAL20
help.avoid: Avoid invented codes or codes that are not active for the stated offer.

variables.label: Offer terms
variables.placeholder: Example: New customers only. One use per customer. Selected services excluded.
variables.hint: Include confirmed eligibility, exclusions, and redemption conditions.

help.what: Include confirmed eligibility, exclusions, and redemption conditions.
help.why: It prevents the generator from guessing important offer restrictions.
help.example: Example: New customers only. One use per customer. Selected services excluded.
help.avoid: Avoid hiding important eligibility, exclusions, limits, fees, or expiry details.

variables.label: Event location
variables.placeholder: Example: Main branch, 24 Green Street, Leeds
variables.hint: Enter the confirmed venue, branch, or online-event location.

help.what: Enter the confirmed venue, branch, or online-event location.
help.why: Customers need accurate attendance information without invented venue details.
help.example: Example: Main branch, 24 Green Street, Leeds
help.avoid: Avoid inventing venues, online links, accessibility arrangements, or capacity.

variables.label: Local context
variables.placeholder: Mention a neighborhood need, local season, branch detail, or community connection.
variables.hint: Add context that makes the post genuinely useful to nearby customers.

help.what: Add context that makes the post genuinely useful to nearby customers.
help.why: It adds authentic geographic relevance beyond simply inserting a city name.
help.example: Mention a neighborhood need, local season, branch detail, or community connection.
help.avoid: Avoid keyword stuffing, fake community references, or locations the business does not serve.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Example: Helpful, straightforward, calm, and never overly promotional
variables.hint: Describe any established communication style the post should follow.

help.what: Describe any established communication style the post should follow.
help.why: It keeps the generated post consistent with the business’s existing communication.
help.example: Example: Helpful, straightforward, calm, and never overly promotional
help.avoid: Avoid voice notes that conflict with factual, local, privacy, or regulated-content requirements.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose a tone
variables.hint: Select the overall feeling without changing the underlying facts.

help.what: Select the overall feeling without changing the underlying facts.
help.why: Tone materially affects how the same factual message is presented.
help.example: Choose a tone
help.avoid: Avoid exaggerated hype, all caps, fake urgency, or excessive promotional language.

variables.label: Words or claims to avoid
variables.placeholder: List prohibited phrases, claims, topics, competitor names, or sensitive wording.
variables.hint: Use this for brand, legal, contractual, or editorial restrictions.

help.what: Use this for brand, legal, contractual, or editorial restrictions.
help.why: The model cannot infer business-specific wording restrictions.
help.example: List prohibited phrases, claims, topics, competitor names, or sensitive wording.
help.avoid: Avoid vague restrictions that contradict required facts or platform-safe wording.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.placeholder: Choose a quantity
variables.hint: Additional variants use different openings while preserving the same facts.

help.what: Additional variants use different openings while preserving the same facts.
help.why: It controls output volume without generating an excessive number of versions.
help.example: Choose a quantity
help.avoid: Avoid requesting synonym-only rewrites; variants should change opening or emphasis meaningfully.

variables.label: Include visual suggestion
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Adds one practical photo or video direction suited to the post.

help.what: Adds one practical photo or video direction suited to the post.
help.why: Visual guidance can improve a local post but is not necessary for every workflow.
help.example: Not applicable
help.avoid: Avoid fake customers, fabricated results, misleading before-and-after visuals, or unowned logos.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining context, formatting preference, or publishing restriction.
variables.hint: Use only for important requirements not covered elsewhere.

help.what: Use only for important requirements not covered elsewhere.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for legitimate edge cases without adding more fields.
help.example: Add any remaining context, formatting preference, or publishing restriction.
help.avoid: Avoid duplicate requirements, unsupported claims, or instructions that conflict with the supplied facts.
